reasoning: The 1-year Treasury rate finished January 2026 at a local minimum of 3.35, having trended in the 3.37-3.42 range for most of the month. Due to short-term mean reversion, there is a relatively high likelihood that it bounces slightly off this floor by the first resolution date (Feb 8), so I assign a 65% probability. For the longer timeframe (March 3), we have to factor in the broader macroeconomic trend of falling interest rates observed over the past year (from over 4.0% down to current levels in 2025-2026). Over a one-month horizon, this secular downward drift offsets short-term mean reversion, dropping the likelihood of an increase back down toward 50%. Therefore, as requested by the prompt, the forecast converges closely to 0.5 for the more distant date.
